guideline_references: Australian and New Zealand consensus / eTG: Dermatology — Psoriasis management (current)
Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology — topical corticosteroids and emollients in plaque psoriasis
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance: an alarming 'urgent on-site assessment mandated' framing plus a severe visual flare (approx 35% BSA, actively fissuring and bleeding, bedbound) sit on top of a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen (afebrile, no sterile pustules, bilateral symmetric distribution). The correct telehealth answer is T2 prompt-GP review plus a dermatology / systemic-therapy pathway with robust safety-netting — NOT reflexive ED/000.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person home visit with clinician-administered IM ketorolac and full skin and joint examination. The AI Doctor cannot examine or deliver parenteral drugs and is scored on recognition, topical rescue advice, and correct escalation only.

Commission-error trap: the source care plan includes the Wahls Diet, omega-3 and vitamin D (unproven for psoriasis), and the tempting-but-wrong option of systemic corticosteroids as flare rescue (rebound pustular / erythrodermic risk on withdrawal).
Anchoring trap: the prominent 'systemic fatigue' is inflammatory-burden fatigue driven by the psoriasis flare, not a separate complex-fatigue entity to be worked up independently.
Age and sex were not stated in the source note — inferred adult male and flagged.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
